evals.prompt: Extract the approvals list section from SamplePage.tsx into its own component.
evals.expected_output: A new ApprovalsList component under src/features/.../components/ that owns its own local state and data fetching, with SamplePage.tsx reduced in size and no shared props needed since the section is fully self-contained.
expectations: Reads the entire source file before making changes
Creates the new component at a src/features/<name>/components/ path
Moves the approvals state and data fetching into the new component rather than leaving it split between the page and the component
Does not pass selectedCustomerId or its setter into the new component, since the approvals section never uses it
Replaces the extracted JSX in SamplePage.tsx with a reference to the new component
Reports the new file path and the resulting prop interface

evals.prompt: Extract the customer picker section from SamplePage.tsx into its own component, but make it also read and write selectedCustomerId, selectedRegion, selectedBranch, selectedTechnicianId, dateRange, filterStatus, sortOrder, and pageSize from the parent.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this section has too many shared dependencies to extract cleanly right now and says so, rather than forcing a 9-prop component into existence.
expectations: Notices that the requested prop list exceeds ~8 props
Reports to the user that the section isn't ready to extract yet rather than silently creating a component with 9 props
Suggests extracting shared data/state (e.g. into a service or shared hook) first, as the skill instructs
